items.name: Dev Environment Setup - macOS
items.notes:
# macOS Development Setup

## Prerequisites
- Homebrew: `/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"`
- Xcode CLI: `xcode-select --install`

## Core Tools
```bash
brew install dotnet-sdk node@20 docker docker-compose rustup-init
brew install --cask visual-studio-code rider
rustup default stable
```

## Database
```bash
docker pull mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
docker run -e 'ACCEPT_EULA=Y' -e 'SA_PASSWORD=S3curePa55w0rd' -p 1433:1433 --name zkl-mssql -d mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
```

## Environment Variables
```bash
export ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Development
export ZKL_DB_CONNECTION='Server=localhost;Database=vault;User Id=sa;Password=S3curePa55w0rd;'
```

## Verify
```bash
dotnet --version && node --version && docker --version && cargo --version
```

items.name: Dev Environment Setup - Linux
items.notes:
# Linux (Ubuntu/Debian) Development Setup

## System Packages
```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y build-essential curl git wget unzip
```

## .NET SDK
```bash
wget https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh -O dotnet-install.sh
chmod +x dotnet-install.sh && ./dotnet-install.sh --channel 8.0
```

## Node.js via nvm
```bash
curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash
nvm install 20 && nvm use 20
```

## Docker
```bash
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

## WSL Notes (Windows Users)
- Use WSL2 with Ubuntu 22.04
- Mount host repos via /mnt/c/Users/...
- Docker Desktop WSL2 backend recommended
- Set `export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock`

items.name: Local Database Setup
items.notes:
# Local MSSQL Database Setup

## Docker Compose (Recommended)
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  mssql:
    image: mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
    environment:
      - ACCEPT_EULA=Y
      - SA_PASSWORD=S3curePa55w0rd
      - MSSQL_PID=Developer
    ports:
      - '1433:1433'
    volumes:
      - mssql-data:/var/opt/mssql
volumes:
  mssql-data:
```

## Connection Strings
- Default: `Server=localhost;Database=vault;User Id=sa;Password=S3curePa55w0rd;TrustServerCertificate=True`
- Named Instance: `Server=localhost\SQLEXPRESS;Database=vault;Integrated Security=True`

## Migrations
```bash
pwsh dev/migrate.ps1
# Or manually:
dotnet run --project util/MsSqlMigratorUtility -- --database vault
```

## Useful Queries
```sql
SELECT name, state_desc FROM sys.databases;
SELECT TABLE_NAME FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE TABLE_TYPE = 'BASE TABLE';
```

items.name: VPN Configuration Notes
items.notes:
# VPN Configuration

## Endpoints
- US-East: `vpn-east.zero-knowledge.example:1194`
- US-West: `vpn-west.zero-knowledge.example:1194`
- EU-Central: `vpn-eu.zero-knowledge.example:1194`

## Certificate Locations
- CA Cert: `~/.config/zkl-vpn/ca.crt`
- Client Cert: `~/.config/zkl-vpn/client.crt`
- Client Key: `~/.config/zkl-vpn/client.key`

## Connection
```bash
sudo openvpn --config ~/.config/zkl-vpn/zkl-prod.ovpn
```

## Troubleshooting
- If DNS resolution fails: `sudo systemctl restart systemd-resolved`
- Cert expired? Re-download from https://vpn-portal.zero-knowledge.example
- Split tunnel: Only traffic to 10.0.0.0/8 routes through VPN

items.name: AWS Access Setup
items.notes:
# AWS Access Configuration

## Profile Setup (~/.aws/config)
```ini
[profile zkl-dev]
region = us-east-1
output = json
role_arn = arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/ZKL-Developer
source_profile = zkl-sso
mfa_serial = arn:aws:iam::123456789012:mfa/your-username

[profile zkl-staging]
region = us-east-1
role_arn = arn:aws:iam::234567890123:role/ZKL-Staging-ReadOnly
source_profile = zkl-sso
```

## SSO Login
```bash
aws sso login --profile zkl-dev
```

## MFA Instructions
1. Install authenticator app (Duo recommended)
2. Go to IAM > Users > your-user > Security Credentials
3. Assign MFA device
4. Use `aws sts get-session-token --serial-number <mfa-arn> --token-code <code>`

## Role Assumption
```bash
aws sts assume-role --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/ZKL-Developer --role-session-name dev-session
```

items.name: GCP Service Account Setup
items.notes:
# GCP Service Account Configuration

## Project IDs
- Dev: `zkl-dev-platform`
- Staging: `zkl-staging-platform`
- Production: `zkl-prod-platform` (restricted access)

## Service Account Key Management
```bash
# Create key (admin only)
gcloud iam service-accounts keys create key.json \
  --iam-account=svc-dev@zkl-dev-platform.iam.gserviceaccount.com

# Activate
gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file=key.json
```

## Application Default Credentials
```bash
gcloud auth application-default login
export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json
```

## Key Rotation Policy
- Keys rotate every 90 days
- Old keys have 7-day grace period
- Notify #platform-eng Slack channel before rotation

items.name: On-Call Runbook
items.notes:
# On-Call Runbook

## Severity Levels
- SEV1 (Critical): Complete service outage, data loss risk. Response: 5 min. Escalate immediately.
- SEV2 (Major): Significant degradation, feature unavailable. Response: 15 min.
- SEV3 (Minor): Minor impact, workaround exists. Response: 1 hour.
- SEV4 (Low): Cosmetic, no user impact. Response: next business day.

## Escalation Chain
1. Primary on-call engineer
2. Secondary on-call engineer (15 min no-response)
3. Engineering Manager (30 min no-response)
4. VP Engineering (SEV1 only, 45 min no-response)
5. CTO (SEV1 with data breach, immediate)

## War Room Setup
1. Create Slack channel: #incident-YYYY-MM-DD-brief-description
2. Start Zoom bridge: On-Call War Room (always-on link)
3. Assign roles: Incident Commander, Scribe, Communications
4. Post status updates every 15 min (SEV1) or 30 min (SEV2)

## Post-Incident
- Blameless postmortem within 48 hours
- Document in Confluence: Engineering > Postmortems
- Action items tracked in Jira epic: INCIDENT-XXXX

items.name: Code Review Checklist
items.notes:
# Code Review Checklist

## Before Requesting Review
- Self-reviewed the diff
- Tests pass locally (dotnet test)
- No secrets or PII in code
- PR description explains WHY, not just WHAT
- Linked Jira ticket in PR title: [ZKL-1234] Description

## Reviewer Checklist
- Logic correctness - does it do what it claims?
- Edge cases - nulls, empty collections, boundaries
- Security - input validation, SQL injection, XSS
- Performance - N+1 queries, unnecessary allocations
- Error handling - appropriate exceptions, logging
- Naming - clear, consistent with codebase conventions
- Tests - adequate coverage, testing behavior not implementation

## Team Conventions
- Approve with 'LGTM' or specific approval
- Request changes with actionable feedback
- Use 'nit:' prefix for non-blocking suggestions
- Minimum 1 approval required, 2 for security-sensitive changes
- Auto-merge enabled for PRs with 2+ approvals and green CI

items.name: New Hire Engineering Onboarding
items.notes:
# New Hire Engineering Onboarding

## Week 1 - Setup & Orientation
- Laptop configured (macOS or Linux)
- Accounts created: GitHub, Jira, Confluence, Slack, Okta, Duo
- Dev environment set up (see 'Dev Environment Setup' notes)
- VPN configured and tested
- AWS/GCP access requested via IT ticket
- Added to team Slack channels
- 1:1 with Engineering Manager
- Meet your onboarding buddy

## Week 2 - Codebase Deep-Dive
- Clone and build all repositories
- Run full test suite successfully
- Review Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
- Shadow a code review
- First 'good first issue' PR submitted

## Week 3-4 - Ramp Up
- Complete first feature PR
- Participate in sprint planning
- Join on-call shadow rotation
- Present at team demo

## Key Contacts
- IT Support: #it-help on Slack
- Security Questions: #security on Slack
- HR/Benefits: #people-ops on Slack

items.name: Release Process
items.notes:
# Release Process

## Branching Strategy
- `main` - always deployable, protected
- `release/vX.Y.Z` - cut from main for release candidates
- `hotfix/description` - emergency fixes branched from latest release tag

## Release Steps
1. Cut release branch: `git checkout -b release/vX.Y.Z main`
2. Bump version numbers in `Directory.Build.props`
3. Run full regression: `dotnet test --configuration Release`
4. Create RC tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z-rc1`
5. Deploy to staging, run smoke tests
6. QA sign-off (72-hour bake period for major releases)
7. Merge to main, create release tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`
8. Deploy to production (blue-green deployment)
9. Monitor dashboards for 1 hour post-deploy
10. Announce in #engineering and #releases

## Rollback Procedure
1. Revert to previous blue/green slot
2. Verify service health
3. Create hotfix branch if needed
4. Post-incident review if rollback was due to bug

items.name: Incident Response Playbook
items.notes:
# Incident Response Playbook

## Detection
- Automated: PagerDuty alerts from Datadog monitors
- Manual: User reports via Zendesk or #incidents Slack channel
- Synthetic: Uptime checks from multiple regions

## Response Steps
1. Acknowledge the alert within SLA
2. Assess severity using the On-Call Runbook severity matrix
3. Communicate - post in #incidents with initial assessment
4. Mitigate - apply immediate fix or workaround
5. Resolve - deploy permanent fix
6. Review - schedule blameless postmortem

## Communication Templates
- Internal: 'Investigating [service] [impact]. Updates every [N] min in #incident-[date]-[desc]'
- External (if user-facing): 'We are aware of issues affecting [feature]. Our team is actively working on resolution. Updates at status.zero-knowledge.example'

## War Room Roles
- Incident Commander: Coordinates response, makes decisions
- Scribe: Documents timeline, actions, decisions
- Communications Lead: Handles stakeholder updates
- Subject Matter Expert: Diagnoses and fixes the issue

items.name: Architecture Decision Records Template
items.notes:
# Architecture Decision Record Template

## ADR-XXXX: [Title]

### Status
[Proposed | Accepted | Deprecated | Superseded by ADR-YYYY]

### Context
What is the issue? What forces are at play? What decision needs to be made?

### Decision
What is the change that we're proposing and/or doing?

### Consequences
Positive:
- ...

Negative:
- ...

Neutral:
- ...

### Alternatives Considered
1. [Alternative A]: Description. Rejected because...
2. [Alternative B]: Description. Rejected because...

---

## Filing Instructions
1. Copy this template
2. Number sequentially (check last ADR number)
3. Submit as PR to `docs/adrs/` directory
4. Require 2 approvals from staff+ engineers
5. Present at Architecture Review meeting

## Where to Find ADRs
- Repository: `github.com/zero-knowledge-labs/architecture`
- Confluence: Engineering > Architecture > Decision Records

items.name: Q3 2024 Infrastructure Audit - CONFIDENTIAL
items.notes:
## Executive Summary
Third-party audit conducted by SecureReview Partners (Sep 2024).

## Key Findings
1. All production databases encrypted at rest (AES-256) - PASS
2. TLS 1.3 enforced on all public endpoints - PASS
3. IAM roles follow least-privilege principle - PASS with minor findings
4. Secret rotation cadence: 87% compliance (target 95%) - NEEDS IMPROVEMENT
5. Container image scanning: All images scanned pre-deployment - PASS
6. Network segmentation between prod/staging - PASS
7. Backup restoration tested quarterly - PASS
8. Employee security training completion: 94% - PASS

## Action Items
- Increase secret rotation compliance to 95% by Q4 2024
- Implement automated rotation for remaining 13% of secrets
- Schedule follow-up audit for Q1 2025

## Classification: Internal - Confidential
Do not share outside of Security Engineering and Executive teams.

items.name: PTO Policy Quick Reference
items.notes:
## PTO Accrual (US Employees)
- 0-2 years: 15 days/year
- 3-5 years: 20 days/year
- 5+ years: 25 days/year
- Sick leave: 10 days/year (separate bank)
- Company holidays: 11 days/year

## International
- Follow local statutory minimums + 5 additional days
- Managed through Deel for contractors

## Approval
- Up to 5 consecutive days: Manager approval
- 5+ consecutive days: Manager + VP approval
- Tracked in BambooHR

items.notes: On-prem Nessus scanner decommissioned Q2 2024. Migrated to Tenable.io cloud. Leaving creds for any leftover scan reports.

items.name: Security Tool Stack Overview
items.notes:
## Current Stack (2025)
- EDR: CrowdStrike Falcon (all endpoints)
- SIEM: Splunk Cloud + Elastic (dual)
- Vuln Management: Tenable.io (infra) + Snyk (code)
- SAST: Checkmarx
- DAST: Burp Suite Enterprise
- Cloud Security: Wiz
- Secrets Management: HashiCorp Vault
- Bug Bounty: HackerOne (primary) + Bugcrowd (secondary)
- Certificate Management: DigiCert
- Threat Intel: VirusTotal Enterprise + MITRE ATT&CK

## Deprecated
- Nessus (replaced by Tenable.io)
- OSSEC (replaced by CrowdStrike)

items.name: Pen Test Schedule 2025
items.notes:
## Scheduled Engagements
- Q1: External network pen test (Cobalt)
- Q2: Web application pen test (internal team + Cobalt)
- Q3: Red team exercise (Cobalt)
- Q4: Cloud infrastructure review (Wiz + internal)

## Annual Requirements
- SOC 2 requires annual pen test
- PCI-DSS requires quarterly vulnerability scan
- Bug bounty program runs continuously
